trellis_version.description: Optional. Semver of the canonical core-rules this clone is pinned to. Read by scripts/upgrade.sh.

presets.description: Optional. List of preset rule-layers to load on top of the parent rules. Each entry must match a file under core-rules/presets/<name>.md. Symlinks land at <project>/.claude/rules/preset-<name>.md and <project>/.agents/rules/preset-<name>.md (under Codex). Order is preserved — later entries compose on top of earlier ones.

approved_mcps.description: Optional. Allowlist of MCP server names sanctioned for use across registered projects. Today this is documentation only — projects MAY connect MCPs not listed here. An operator audit may warn when a project's .mcp.json references an unapproved server. Names match the server name as exposed to the harness (e.g., the prefix before '__' in mcp__<server>__<tool>). Examples: 'operator-scheduler', 'computer-use', 'claude-in-chrome', 'Vercel', 'Cloudflare', 'obsidian'.

scope.description: 'fleet' = enabled across all registered projects; 'per-project' = only where the project explicitly opts in.

autonomy_default.description: Optional. Fleet default autonomy level (integer 1–5) used by every session under this clone unless overridden. 1=pedagogical, 2=cautious, 3=standard (default), 4=initiative, 5=autonomous. Project-local <project>/.trellis.config.json may override with its own 'autonomy' field. Presets may clamp via 'autonomy_ceiling'.

autonomy.description: Optional. Project-local override of autonomy_default. Same range and semantics. Used only in project-local <project>/.trellis.config.json files, but accepted at fleet-level for symmetry.

package_manager.description: Optional. Default JS package manager for hooks and gates that run project scripts (pre-push, stop-verify, process-gate). 'auto' (the default when unset) = detect from lockfile: pnpm-lock.yaml→pnpm, bun.lock(b)→bun, yarn.lock→yarn, package-lock.json→npm. An explicit value (pnpm|npm|bun|yarn) forces that manager regardless of lockfile. Project-local <project>/.trellis.config.json may override with its own 'package_manager'. With the key unset, resolution is byte-identical to pre-config Trellis (pure lockfile detection) — this key is purely additive.

disk_janitor.description: Optional. Tuning for `trellis disk-janitor`. Every key is optional; the orchestrator fills per-key defaults when the key or the whole object is absent (enabled true; cache_ttl_days 14; worktree_stale_days 30; free_space_floor_gb 30; cache_ceiling_gb 20; skip_projects []). Absence of the object is byte-identical to the defaults — this key is purely additive.

enabled.description: Master switch for the disk-janitor scans. Default true.

cache_ttl_days.description: A build-cache directory is a prune candidate only when its mtime is older than this many days. Default 14.

worktree_stale_days.description: A worktree is a reap candidate only when its last commit is older than this many days (one of four gates). Default 30.

free_space_floor_gb.description: Tripwire: report flags low disk when free space on the projects volume drops below this many GB. Default 30.

cache_ceiling_gb.description: Tripwire: report flags a runaway cache when the largest single build cache exceeds this many GB. Default 20.

skip_projects.description: Registry project names the janitor never scans or touches, on top of blacklist.md. Default [].

codex_fanout.description: Optional central configuration for the codex-fanout recipe. Callers resolve concurrency from this block and pass it explicitly as args.codexCap; the recipe has no baked fallback. When this block is present, concurrency is required and must be a positive integer. The public example uses 4 as the fleet baseline.

concurrency.description: Maximum Codex generation units admitted to one fan-out wave. Claude units do not consume this cap. Must be resolved by the caller and passed as codexCap.

loop_safety.description: Optional. Instance baseline for the loop-safety contract (core-rules/loop-safety.md). Every Trellis loop declares and honors three ceilings and halts on ANY one. Every key is optional; absence of a key or of the whole object falls back to the documented built-in fallback constants identical to these baselines (max_iterations 100; no_progress_iterations 3; budget_ceiling_usd 1000; usd_per_mtok 25.00). The optional codex_usd_per_mtok has no baseline constant: absent, cross-harness Codex spend is attributed at usd_per_mtok (backward compatible). Resolution, most specific wins: per-loop `safety` override → project-local <project>/.trellis.config.json.loop_safety → this central block → built-in fallback constants. Absence of the object is byte-identical to the defaults — this key is purely additive.

max_iterations.description: Hard cap on loop iterations / agent-dispatch rounds; the loop halts when reached. Complements (does not replace) the Workflow engine's 1000-agent lifetime backstop. Default 100.

no_progress_iterations.description: Halt after this many consecutive iterations that make no measurable progress, where 'progress' is the loop's declared progress signal (commit/PR, file delta, new finding, work-list drain, or state-hash change as catch-all). Default 3.

budget_ceiling_usd.description: Spend ceiling per loop run, in US dollars; the loop halts when reached. Mapped onto the Workflow engine's token-native budget.total via usd_per_mtok. Default 1000.

usd_per_mtok.description: US dollars per million output tokens, used to convert budget_ceiling_usd to the engine's token-native budget (budget_tokens = round(budget_ceiling_usd / usd_per_mtok * 1_000_000)). Tracks the loop model's output price; update here when pricing changes. Default 25.00 (Claude Opus 4.8 output, $25/MTok).

codex_usd_per_mtok.description: Optional. US dollars per million output tokens for the GPT-5.x / Codex model, used by cross-harness loops so Codex-unit spend is attributed at the Codex rate rather than the Opus rate when mapping budget_ceiling_usd onto the engine's token budget. Absent → Codex spend falls back to usd_per_mtok (backward compatible). Note: Codex's per-task cost win is mostly ~3-4x fewer tokens, not a lower per-MTok rate; set this to Codex's real output price, not a fudge factor.

mandatory_pipeline.description: Optional. The mandatory feature-pipeline gate (spec 006; core-rules/hooks.md). When enabled, a branch whose net gated diff vs the protected merge-base (excluding tests/docs/specs/audits/lockfiles/generated/operational paths) exceeds spec_required_diff_lines cannot be pushed unless ONE of three routes is satisfied: a spec triad added on THIS branch (+ an interview artifact), a size-capped `/surgical` declaration (net gated diff ≤ surgical_max_diff_lines), or a logged `/surgical --emergency` override. Enforced deterministically at pre-push — a pure function of git/filesystem state, so it is equal on Claude and Codex by construction — with a Stop-hook early-warning. Every key is optional; absence of the object, or enabled:false, is byte-identical to prior behavior (default OFF). A present-but-malformed block fails CLOSED (an opted-in project must not be silently disabled by a typo). Resolution, most specific wins: project-local <project>/.trellis.config.json.mandatory_pipeline → this central block → built-in (off, floor 80, ceiling 400).

enabled.description: Master switch for the mandatory-pipeline gate. Default false — absence or false is byte-identical to pre-006 behavior.

spec_required_diff_lines.description: Size floor: net gated diff (added+deleted, excluded paths removed) at or below this many lines is treated as sub-floor and passes with no spec (surgical-by-default). Above it, the branch must take one of the three routes. Default 80.

surgical_max_diff_lines.description: Surgical ceiling: a `/surgical` declaration is honored only while the net gated diff stays at or below this many lines. Grow past it and the marker is invalid — the gate re-blocks and audit-logs an `oversized-surgical` line; use `/surgical --emergency` for genuine over-cap urgency. Default 400.
